pyMediaManager
  • API Reference
  • Core Services
  • View page source

Core Services

Logging Service

Logging service for pyMediaManager. Provides rich console logging and rotating file logs.

class app.core.logging_service.LoggingService[source]

Bases: object

Service for configuring application logging with console and file outputs.

__init__(app_name='pyMediaManager', log_dir=None, level='INFO', console_enabled=True, file_enabled=True, max_file_size=10485760, backup_count=5, file_system_service=None)[source]

Initialize logging service.

Parameters:

  • app_name (str) – Application name for logger

  • log_dir (Path | None) – Directory for log files (if None, uses portable folder from file_system_service)

  • level (str) – Logging level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL)

  • console_enabled (bool) – Enable console logging with rich formatting

  • file_enabled (bool) – Enable file logging with rotation

  • max_file_size (int) – Maximum log file size in bytes before rotation

  • backup_count (int) – Number of backup log files to keep

  • file_system_service (object | None) – FileSystemService instance for portable folder detection

setup()[source]

Setup logging configuration with console and file handlers.

Returns:

Configured logger instance

Return type:

Logger

get_logger(name=None)[source]

Get a logger instance.

Parameters:

name (str | None) – Logger name (defaults to app_name)

Returns:

Logger instance

Return type:

Logger

set_level(level)[source]

Change logging level dynamically.

Parameters:

level (str) – New logging level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL)

get_log_file_path()[source]

Get the current log file path.

Returns:

Path to log file or None if file logging disabled

Return type:

Path | None

get_all_log_files()[source]

Get all log files (current and rotated).

Returns:

List of log file paths

Return type:

list[Path]

Configuration Service

Configuration service for pyMediaManager. Handles layered configuration (defaults → environment → user) with Pydantic models.

class app.core.services.config_service.LogLevel[source]

Bases: str, Enum

Logging levels.

DEBUG = 'DEBUG'
INFO = 'INFO'
WARNING = 'WARNING'
ERROR = 'ERROR'
CRITICAL = 'CRITICAL'
__new__(value)
class app.core.services.config_service.PathConfig[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Configuration for application paths.

projects_dir: str
logs_dir: str
plugins_dir: str
config_dir: str
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class app.core.services.config_service.LoggingConfig[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Configuration for logging.

level: LogLevel
console_enabled: bool
file_enabled: bool
max_file_size: int
backup_count: int
format: str
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class app.core.services.config_service.UIConfig[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Configuration for UI settings.

theme: str
show_first_run: bool
window_width: int
window_height: int
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class app.core.services.config_service.PluginConfig[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Configuration for plugin system.

auto_update_check: bool
download_timeout: int
parallel_downloads: int
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class app.core.services.config_service.AppConfig[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Main application configuration.

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {'extra': 'allow'}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

app_name: str
app_version: str
app_commit: str | None
paths: PathConfig
logging: LoggingConfig
ui: UIConfig
plugins: PluginConfig
to_dict(redact_sensitive=False)[source]

Convert config to dictionary.

Parameters:

redact_sensitive (bool) – If True, replace sensitive values with [REDACTED]

Returns:

Dictionary representation of config

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

model_post_init(context, /)

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.

Parameters:

  • self (BaseModel) – The BaseModel instance.

  • context (Any) – The context.

class app.core.services.config_service.ConfigService[source]

Bases: object

Service for managing layered application configuration.

__init__(app_root, config_dir=None)[source]

Initialize configuration service.

Parameters:

  • app_root (Path) – Application root directory

  • config_dir (Path | None) – Configuration directory (defaults to app_root/config)

load()[source]

Load configuration from all layers (defaults → file → environment → user).

Returns:

Loaded AppConfig object

Return type:

AppConfig

save_user_config(config)[source]

Save user configuration to user.yaml.

Parameters:

config (AppConfig) – AppConfig object to save

get_config()[source]

Get current configuration, loading it if not already loaded.

Returns:

Current AppConfig object

Return type:

AppConfig

update_config(**kwargs)[source]

Update configuration values and save to user config.

Parameters:

**kwargs (Any) – Configuration values to update

Returns:

Updated AppConfig object

Return type:

AppConfig

reset_to_defaults()[source]

Reset configuration to defaults by removing user config.

Returns:

Default AppConfig object

Return type:

AppConfig

export_config(output_path, redact_sensitive=True)[source]

Export current configuration to a file.

Parameters:

  • output_path (Path) – Path to output file

  • redact_sensitive (bool) – If True, redact sensitive values

File System Service

File system service for pyMediaManager. Provides abstraction layer for file operations with portable path handling.

class app.core.services.file_system_service.FileSystemService[source]

Bases: object

Service for file system operations with portable path handling.

__init__(app_root=None)[source]

Initialize file system service.

Parameters:

app_root (Path | None) – Application root directory. If None, auto-detects from module path.

get_app_root()[source]

Get the application root directory.

get_drive_root()[source]

Get the root of the drive containing the app.

Returns the drive root (e.g., D:if app is at D:pyMM). Caches the result after first call.

Returns:

Path to drive root directory

Return type:

Path

get_portable_folder(folder_name)[source]

Get path to a portable folder at the drive root.

For portable operation, certain folders (Projects, Logs) should be at the drive root rather than within the app directory.

Parameters:

folder_name (str) – Name of the folder (e.g., “pyMM.Projects”, “pyMM.Logs”)

Returns:

Path to the portable folder at drive root

Return type:

Path

ensure_portable_folders()[source]

Ensure all portable folders exist at the drive root.

Creates pyMM.Projects and pyMM.Logs folders at the root of the drive containing the application.

Returns:

Dictionary mapping folder names to their resolved paths

Return type:

dict[str, Path]

get_relative_path(path, base=None)[source]

Get path relative to base directory.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – Absolute path to convert

  • base (Path | None) – Base directory (defaults to app_root)

Returns:

Relative path from base to path

Return type:

Path

resolve_path(path, relative_to=None)[source]

Resolve a path that may be relative or absolute.

Parameters:

  • path (Path | str) – Path to resolve (string or Path object)

  • relative_to (Path | None) – Base directory for relative paths (defaults to app_root)

Returns:

Resolved absolute path

Return type:

Path

ensure_directory(path)[source]

Ensure a directory exists, creating it if necessary.

Parameters:

path (Path | str) – Directory path to create

Returns:

Resolved path to the directory

Raises:

OSError – If directory creation fails

Return type:

Path

list_directory(path, pattern='*', recursive=False)[source]

List files in a directory matching a pattern.

Parameters:

  • path (Path | str) – Directory to list

  • pattern (str) – Glob pattern to match (default: “*”)

  • recursive (bool) – If True, search recursively with rglob

Returns:

List of matching paths

Return type:

list[Path]

copy_file(src, dst, overwrite=False)[source]

Copy a file from source to destination.

Parameters:

  • src (Path | str) – Source file path

  • dst (Path | str) – Destination file path

  • overwrite (bool) – If True, overwrite existing destination

Returns:

Path to the copied file

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If source doesn’t exist

  • FileExistsError – If destination exists and overwrite=False

Return type:

Path

move_file(src, dst, overwrite=False)[source]

Move a file from source to destination.

Parameters:

  • src (Path | str) – Source file path

  • dst (Path | str) – Destination file path

  • overwrite (bool) – If True, overwrite existing destination

Returns:

Path to the moved file

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If source doesn’t exist

  • FileExistsError – If destination exists and overwrite=False

Return type:

Path

delete_file(path, missing_ok=True)[source]

Delete a file.

Parameters:

  • path (Path | str) – File path to delete

  • missing_ok (bool) – If True, don’t raise error if file doesn’t exist

Returns:

True if file was deleted, False if it didn’t exist

Raises:

FileNotFoundError – If file doesn’t exist and missing_ok=False

Return type:

bool

delete_directory(path, missing_ok=True, recursive=False)[source]

Delete a directory.

Parameters:

  • path (Path | str) – Directory path to delete

  • missing_ok (bool) – If True, don’t raise error if directory doesn’t exist

  • recursive (bool) – If True, delete recursively (rmtree)

Returns:

True if directory was deleted, False if it didn’t exist

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If directory doesn’t exist and missing_ok=False

  • OSError – If directory is not empty and recursive=False

Return type:

bool

get_file_size(path)[source]

Get file size in bytes.

Parameters:

path (Path | str) – File path

Returns:

File size in bytes

Raises:

FileNotFoundError – If file doesn’t exist

Return type:

int

get_free_space(path)[source]

Get free disk space for the drive containing the path.

Parameters:

path (Path | str) – Any path on the drive

Returns:

Free space in bytes

Return type:

int

Storage Service

Storage service for detecting and managing portable drives.

class app.core.services.storage_service.DriveInfo[source]

Bases: object

Information about a storage drive.

drive_letter: str
label: str
file_system: str
total_size: int
free_space: int
is_removable: bool
serial_number: str | None = None
property used_space: int

Calculate used space in bytes.

property used_percent: float

Calculate used space as percentage.

__init__(drive_letter, label, file_system, total_size, free_space, is_removable, serial_number=None)
class app.core.services.storage_service.StorageService[source]

Bases: object

Service for detecting and managing portable storage drives.

__init__()[source]

Initialize storage service.

get_all_drives()[source]

Get information about all mounted drives.

Returns:

List of DriveInfo objects for all drives

Return type:

list[DriveInfo]

get_removable_drives()[source]

Get information about removable drives only.

Returns:

List of DriveInfo objects for removable drives

Return type:

list[DriveInfo]

get_drive_info(path)[source]

Get drive information for the drive containing the given path.

Parameters:

path (Path | str) – Any path on the drive

Returns:

DriveInfo object or None if drive not found

Return type:

DriveInfo | None

is_path_on_removable_drive(path)[source]

Check if a path is on a removable drive.

Parameters:

path (Path | str) – Path to check

Returns:

True if path is on a removable drive

Return type:

bool

get_drive_root(path)[source]

Get the root path of the drive containing the given path.

Parameters:

path (Path | str) – Any path on the drive

Returns:

Path object pointing to drive root, or None if not found

Return type:

Path | None
